REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1,700,000.00)
Off Calendar Oct-14-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw appeared by zoom for and with Barbara Simon (Administrator) by zoom.
David Yang appeared by zoom for and with Gayle Mazza (Beneficiary) by telephone.
Norman Reitz appeared by telephone for Will Contestants.
Nancy Hill in Propria Persona.
Tim Curran (listing Agent) in Propria Persona.

Ms. Hinshaw stated that the Buyer pulled out of the sale because of the objection. Ms. Hill stated that there is an issue with the sale price, spoke of a prior estimate of the property that was provided by an independent Realtor in 2016, stated that $100,000.00 was put into the property for staging and that a single family dwelling nearby is selling for $3,000,000.00. Ms. Hill also stated that she has concerns because the listing Agent and the Buyer's Agent are from the real estate company, that there is an issue of how title is being held, that she does not know who's buying the property, that if it is bought by a Trust, they are going to sell it and that the parties have not been fair. The Court explained the confirmation of sale process. Ms. Hill expressed concern about the lost sale and stated that the objections are hampering the estate. The Court stated that it does not find Ms. Hill's objection of sufficient merit to stand in the way of the sale of the property, that if an objection raises issues of something improper, it will take a look at it, explained the overbidding process and overrules Ms. Hill's objections. In response to Ms. Hinshaw's request for the Court to order Ms. Hill to provide a tax identification number, the Court stated that it does not believe it can order Ms. Hill to provide that information and that Ms. Hinshaw can come back to Court after the sale. Ms. Hinshaw stated that Ms. Hill was ordered to to file written objections and that the objections have not been filed under the code. The Court stated that Ms. Hill has the right to present oral objections. Mr. Curran stated that the Buyer made offers on two other properties and got them both, and the Buyer used the money to purchase the other properties and indicated that they won't be involved in this sale. Mr. Curran also offered to speak with Ms. Hill out of Court and assist in providing clarification. The Court encouraged Ms. Hill to speak with Mr. Curran. The Report of Sale and Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property is off calendar. The Petition for Instructions will be granted in part and denied in part, and is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Probate Code Section 11604 Petition To Deduct The Fees And Costs Incurred By The Petitioners From The Distributions Otherwise Payable To Respondents Gayle Mazza And Nancy Hill For Their Breaches Of The Stipulation For Settlement, And To Pay Those Fees And Costs To The Petitioners Attorneys
Off Calendar Sep-16-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Norman Reitz and Walter Cook for Petitioners.
David Yang for and with Gayle Mazza (appeared by telephone).
Nancy Hill in Propria Persona.

Ms. Hill stated that she does not have legal Counsel and that she did not think it was urgent because the house hasn't been sold. The Court informed Ms. Hill that it is not inclined to continue the matter and noted that there have been four continuances in four months. Ms. Hill stated that she called Attorney Steve Weinkauf about a week ago and that she called some law firms, but does not have specific dates as to when she called. Mr. Reitz stated that Ms. Hill has abundantly been given time from the Court, and that the matter is ripe and should be heard. Mr. Yang stated that he agrees and that the matter should be adjudicated. The Court stated that it does not think there has been due diligence by Ms. Hill and moves forward with the petition. The Court reads language from paragraph 18 of the settlement agreement, spoke of the process of requesting fees and informed Counsel of its tentative ruling to deny the petition. Mr. Cook stated that the carve out was for doing the work to get the petition for approval of the settlement before the Court, that there was a stipulation/agreement, and that they (Hill and Mazza) breached the agreement and instead filed an opposition. Mr. Yang stated that the Court does not have jurisdiction to approve fees under Probate Code 11604. Ms. Hill stated that she agrees with the Court. The Petition to Deduct Fees and Costs, etc. is denied.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not CCP 664.6 applies to this case and whether or not the signatures are valid. Mr. Bryan will provide a proposed order for probate reflecting appointment of Barbara Simon as Administrator with will annexed.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joseph Barton for Gayle Mazza & Nancy Hill. Norman Reitz for Kathy Ajari.

The Court disclosed that it knows Mr. Barton but that today will be about procedural issues only. Mr. Reitz stated that they are active in discovery. The Court asked if the parties were willing to mediate. Mr. Barton stated no, that they tried to settle but it fell through and now they are in discovery. Mr. Reitz stated that they have depositions and interrogatories scheduled. The Court stated that they should give mediation some thought. Mr. Barton stated that they are not opposed to mediation. Mr. Reitz stated that they are procedurally cured on the petitions, that the are contesting and would like to continue with discovery. The Court stated that the parties need to conclude discovery before they get a trial date, that there is a discovery motion set for December 21, 2016, that these petitions will be continued to a date after that and suggested that the parties start mediation on their own. Norman Reitz for counter-petitioners/objectors.

Mr. Reitz informed the Court that he had filed a will contest/counter petition which was set for hearing on July 6, 2016, and requested that a special administrator be appointed. Mr. Barton stated that his clients are seeking appointment as specified under the terms of the will, and are prepared to be bonded in the sum of $3,600,000. Mr. Reitz responded that the purported will being offered by petitioners was a hand-written will in the handwriting of one of the designated co-administrators, executed 3 days prior to the death of the testator and while the testator was under heavy sedation. He asserted that the will was very suspect. The Court vacated the July 6 hearing date on the counter-petition, and continued both petitions for status hearing on September 12, 2016, at 9:00 am in Department 206.
